title: CBSE Class 12 Previous Year Question Papers (2015-2025) PDF Download
author: PrepOne Academic Team
seoDescription: Download the last 10 years of CBSE Class 12 board exam papers for Physics, Chemistry, Maths, Biology, English and more. Free PDF download with solutions and chapter-wise analysis for 2026 board exam prep.
content:
If you are appearing for the CBSE Class 12 Board Exams in 2026, solving Previous Year Questions (PYQs) is no longer optional — it is the single most important part of your preparation strategy. Students who practice PYQs consistently score 15–20% higher on average than those who don't.

Why Practice from PYQs?

According to our analysis of over 10,000 CBSE questions across 10 years, more than 60% of questions in any given board exam are directly repeated or slightly rephrased from previous years. The CBSE board follows a highly predictable question pattern, making PYQ practice the highest ROI activity in your study plan.

  • Understand exact CBSE marking scheme and answer format
  • Identify high-weightage chapters across subjects
  • Build exam temperament and time management skills
  • Spot recurring question types in Maths, Physics, and Chemistry

Subject-wise PYQ Availability (2015–2025)

PrepOne provides chapter-tagged, searchable PYQs for all major CBSE Class 12 subjects:

SubjectYears AvailableWith Solutions
Physics2015–2025✓ Yes
Chemistry2015–2025✓ Yes
Mathematics2015–2025✓ Yes
Biology2015–2025✓ Yes
English Core2018–2025✓ Yes
Accountancy2017–2025Partial

How PrepOne Makes PYQ Practice Smarter

Downloading a PDF is just the first step. What sets PrepOne apart is its AI-powered adaptive practice engine. Instead of solving papers randomly, PrepOne identifies your weak chapters using IRT (Item Response Theory) and auto-assigns the most relevant PYQs for your current level.

💡 PrepOne Insight: Students who use adaptive PYQ practice on PrepOne reduce their revision time by 40% while improving their score by an average of 18 marks in mock tests.

Top High-Weightage Chapters (Based on 10-Year Analysis)

Our data team analysed CBSE papers from 2015 to 2025 to identify the chapters that appear most consistently:

⚛️ Physics

  • • Electrostatics & Current Electricity (18 marks avg)
  • • Optics — Ray & Wave (14 marks avg)
  • • Semiconductor Devices (8 marks avg)

🧪 Chemistry

  • • Electrochemistry & Chemical Kinetics (15 marks avg)
  • • Coordination Compounds (8 marks avg)
  • • Biomolecules & Polymers (10 marks avg)

📐 Mathematics

  • • Calculus — Integrals & Differentials (35 marks avg)
  • • Vectors & 3D Geometry (14 marks avg)
  • • Probability (10 marks avg)

🌿 Biology

  • • Genetics & Evolution (18 marks avg)
  • • Reproduction (14 marks avg)
  • • Biotechnology (12 marks avg)

Frequently Asked Questions

Are CBSE 2025 board papers available on PrepOne?

Yes. CBSE 2025 board exam papers for all major subjects are available on PrepOne, complete with official marking schemes and AI-generated detailed solutions.

Can I download PYQs as PDF for offline use?

PrepOne allows PDF export of curated question sets. You can also practice offline via our mobile app, which syncs your progress when reconnected.

How is PrepOne different from downloading PDFs from CBSE website?

Raw PDFs give you the paper but no intelligence. PrepOne tags every question by chapter, difficulty, and bloom's level — and tracks your performance over time to guide what you should practise next.

title: Bihar Board Class 12 Important Questions 2026 — Chapter-wise PDF
author: Anmol Kumar
seoDescription: Get the chapter-wise most important questions for Bihar Board Class 12 (BSEB) 2026. Covers Physics, Chemistry, Maths, Biology with expected question types and marks distribution.
content:
The Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) has been significantly evolving its exam pattern over the last three years. If you're preparing for Bihar Board Class 12 exams in 2026, relying on old study material alone won't cut it. This guide gives you a curated list of the most important questions, chapter-wise, based on the latest BSEB trends and PrepOne's question analysis engine.

⚠️ 2026 Pattern Change Alert: BSEB Class 12 has increased the weightage of objective-type questions from 40% to 50% starting 2025. Practising MCQs is now more critical than ever.

How Bihar Board 2026 Exams Are Structured

Understanding the exam structure helps you allocate time efficiently. Bihar Board Class 12 papers follow this general format across Science stream subjects:

  • Section A: Objective MCQs — 50 marks (1 mark each)
  • Section B: Short answer questions — 30 marks (2–3 marks each)
  • Section C: Long answer / descriptive — 20 marks (5 marks each)

Chapter-wise Important Questions — Physics

Based on BSEB papers from 2020–2025 and PrepOne's frequency analysis, here are the highest-priority Physics chapters:

Chapter 1: Electric Charges & Fields

Very High Priority
  • • Derive expression for electric field due to a uniformly charged infinite plane sheet
  • • State and prove Gauss's theorem. Give two applications.
  • • A charge q is placed at the centre of a cube — find flux through each face (MCQ favourite)

Chapter 3: Current Electricity

Very High Priority
  • • Derive the condition for balance in a Wheatstone bridge
  • • Explain the working of a potentiometer with diagram. How is it superior to a voltmeter?
  • • Numerical: internal resistance calculation using potentiometer

Chapter 9: Ray Optics

High Priority
  • • Derive lens maker's formula
  • • Explain total internal reflection. Give two real-world applications.
  • • Numerical: combination of lenses / mirrors

Chapter-wise Important Questions — Chemistry

Chapter 2: Solutions

Very High Priority
  • • Define molality and molarity. How are they related?
  • • Explain Henry's law and its applications
  • • Numerical: elevation in boiling point / depression in freezing point

Chapter 4: Chemical Kinetics

Very High Priority
  • • Derive the integrated rate law for a first-order reaction
  • • What is activation energy? Explain with Arrhenius equation.
  • • Numerical: half-life calculations

Chapter-wise Important Questions — Mathematics

Calculus: Integrals

Very High Priority (20+ marks)
  • • Integration by parts — at least 2 questions guaranteed
  • • Definite integrals using properties (appear every year)
  • • Area under curves using definite integration

Vectors & 3D Geometry

High Priority (14 marks)
  • • Angle between two lines/planes using direction cosines
  • • Shortest distance between skew lines
  • • Dot and cross product applications (work done, area of parallelogram)

PrepOne's Bihar Board Practice Mode

PrepOne has a dedicated Bihar Board (BSEB) practice mode that simulates the actual exam interface, including the OMR-style objective section. Our AI evaluator scores your subjective answers instantly — giving you feedback on content accuracy, keyword coverage, and presentation quality.

1,200+

BSEB-tagged questions

6 yrs

of BSEB PYQ coverage

AI

Subjective answer grading

Frequently Asked Questions

When will BSEB Class 12 2026 exams be held?

BSEB Class 12 exams are typically held in February. The 2026 schedule is expected to be released on the official BSEB website by November 2025. PrepOne will notify students on the app as soon as dates are announced.

How many marks are needed to pass Bihar Board Class 12?

Students need a minimum of 33% in each subject and in aggregate to pass. However, for First Division you need 45%+, and for distinction 60%+.

Is PrepOne free for Bihar Board students?

Yes — PrepOne's core PYQ practice and chapter-wise tests are free. Premium features like AI answer evaluation, full-length mock tests, and personalised study plans require a subscription.

Crack Bihar Board 2026 with PrepOne

Adaptive practice. AI grading. Real BSEB question papers. All in one app.

Start Free Practice →

title: Free JEE Mains Mock Tests 2026 — Full Syllabus Practice with AI Analysis
author: PrepOne Academic Team
seoDescription: Attempt free full-length JEE Mains 2026 mock tests with AI-powered performance analysis. Chapter-wise practice for Physics, Chemistry, and Maths with adaptive difficulty.
content:
Getting into an IIT or NIT starts with clearing JEE Mains — and clearing JEE Mains in 2026 requires a preparation strategy that goes beyond just reading NCERT. Full-length mock tests with detailed analysis are what separate the top 1% from the rest. PrepOne's JEE mock test series is designed by IITians and powered by AI to give you the most exam-realistic practice available online, for free.

JEE Mains 2026 — Key Exam Details

ParameterDetails
Conducting BodyNTA (National Testing Agency)
SessionsSession 1 (January) + Session 2 (April)
Total Questions90 (75 attempted) — Physics, Chemistry, Maths
Total Marks300 marks
Marking Scheme+4 correct, −1 wrong (MCQ); +4, no negative (Integer type)
ModeComputer-Based Test (CBT)

Why Mock Tests Matter More Than Textbooks

Most JEE aspirants spend 90% of their time studying and 10% testing. Research on exam performance consistently shows the opposite is optimal — especially in the final 3 months. Mock tests help you in three ways textbooks can't:

  • Simulate exam pressure — timed 3-hour tests train your brain to perform under stress
  • Expose hidden weak spots — you think you know Thermodynamics until you solve 5 questions back-to-back under time pressure
  • Improve speed and accuracy — the only way to get faster is to actually take timed tests, not read about techniques

💡 PrepOne Data: Students who take 15+ full-length mocks before JEE Session 1 score an average of 22 marks higher compared to those who take fewer than 5.

What's Inside PrepOne's JEE Mock Tests

📋 Test Features

  • ✓ NTA-replica interface with same layout and timer
  • ✓ Section-switching with review marking (just like real exam)
  • ✓ Integer-type questions with virtual numpad
  • ✓ Auto-scored with detailed solutions after submission

🤖 AI Analysis Features

  • ✓ Accuracy % per chapter and per subject
  • ✓ Speed analysis (time per question)
  • ✓ Difficulty-adjusted performance score (IRT-based)
  • ✓ Personalised next-practice recommendations

High-Weightage JEE Mains Topics (Based on 2019–2025 Analysis)

⚛️ Physics

  • • Mechanics (NLM, Work-Energy) — 8–10 Q
  • • Electrostatics + Current Electricity — 7–8 Q
  • • Modern Physics — 3–4 Q
  • • SHM & Waves — 3 Q

🧪 Chemistry

  • • Organic — Reaction mechanisms — 8–9 Q
  • • Physical Chem — Equilibrium, Kinetics — 7 Q
  • • Inorganic — p-block, d-block — 6 Q
  • • Coordination Compounds — 2–3 Q

📐 Mathematics

  • • Calculus (Limits, Derivatives, Integration) — 10 Q
  • • Algebra (Matrices, Complex Numbers) — 7 Q
  • • Coordinate Geometry — 6 Q
  • • Probability & Statistics — 3–4 Q

JEE Preparation Timeline with PrepOne

Aug–Oct

Foundation Phase

Complete NCERT for all subjects. Attempt chapter-wise tests on PrepOne after each topic.

Nov–Dec

Practice Phase

Attempt 2 full-length mocks per week. Use PrepOne's AI analysis to fix weak chapters.

Jan

Revision Phase

Daily mock tests + revision of PrepOne's AI-identified weak areas. Focus on accuracy over speed.

Frequently Asked Questions

How many free mock tests does PrepOne offer for JEE?

PrepOne offers 5 free full-length JEE Mains mock tests. With a premium subscription, you get access to 50+ full-length mocks, 200+ chapter tests, and unlimited PYQ practice.

Are the PrepOne mock tests aligned with the latest NTA pattern?

Yes. PrepOne's question bank and mock test structure are updated within 2 weeks of every official NTA notification. Our team of IIT alumni reviews all questions for accuracy and difficulty calibration.

Does PrepOne have mock tests for JEE Advanced too?

JEE Advanced mock tests are currently in beta on PrepOne and will be fully launched by August 2026. You can join the waitlist from the app.

title: CBSE Class 10 Science Important Questions 2026 — Chapter-wise with Answers
author: PrepOne Academic Team
seoDescription: Chapter-wise most important questions for CBSE Class 10 Science 2026 board exam. Covers Physics, Chemistry, Biology with expected 3-mark and 5-mark questions based on 10-year PYQ analysis.
content:
CBSE Class 10 Science is one of the most scoring subjects in the board exam — if you know where to focus. With 80 marks on the line and a syllabus spanning Physics, Chemistry, and Biology, students who practice strategically consistently outperform those who read every chapter equally. This guide gives you the chapter-wise most important questions for CBSE Class 10 Science 2026, compiled from PrepOne's analysis of 10 years of board papers.

📌 Quick Stat: Our analysis of CBSE Science papers from 2015–2025 shows that just 8 out of 16 chapters contribute to over 70% of the total marks every year. Knowing which 8 to prioritise is half the battle.

CBSE Class 10 Science — Exam Pattern 2026

SectionQuestion TypeMarks
Section AMCQs & Assertion-Reason (1 mark each)20 marks
Section BShort Answer I — 2 marks each20 marks
Section CShort Answer II — 3 marks each24 marks
Section DLong Answer — 5 marks each16 marks

Note: An internal choice is provided in Section B, C, and D. The practical-based questions in Section A carry significant weight — don't skip them.

Physics — Important Questions

Chapter 1: Chemical Reactions & Equations

Very High Priority

Appears in 9 out of 10 years with a 3-mark or 5-mark question

  • Q1.What is a balanced chemical equation? Balance the following: Fe + H₂O → Fe₃O₄ + H₂. (3 marks — asked 2022, 2020, 2018)
  • Q2.With one example each, distinguish between exothermic and endothermic reactions. (3 marks)
  • Q3.Define oxidation and reduction. Identify the oxidising and reducing agent in: CuO + H₂ → Cu + H₂O. (2 marks)

Chapter 12: Electricity

Very High Priority

Consistently 8–10 marks in every board paper

  • Q1.State Ohm's Law. Draw a circuit diagram to verify it and plot the V-I graph. (5 marks)
  • Q2.Three resistors of 5Ω, 10Ω, and 15Ω are connected in parallel. Find the equivalent resistance and total current if connected to a 30V source. (3 marks — Numerical)
  • Q3.Why are domestic circuits connected in parallel? Give two advantages. (2 marks)
  • Q4.What is the commercial unit of electrical energy? Calculate the cost of running a 1500W geyser for 2 hours daily for 30 days at ₹6/unit. (3 marks)

Chapter 13: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current

High Priority
  • Q1.Draw the magnetic field lines around a current-carrying straight conductor. State the right-hand thumb rule. (3 marks)
  • Q2.Explain the working principle of an electric motor with a labelled diagram. (5 marks — asked 4 of last 6 years)
  • Q3.What is electromagnetic induction? State Fleming's Right Hand Rule. (2 marks)

Chemistry — Important Questions

Chapter 2: Acids, Bases and Salts

Very High Priority
  • Q1.Why does dry HCl gas not change the colour of dry litmus paper but HCl solution does? (2 marks — asked 2024, 2021, 2019)
  • Q2.What happens when sodium hydrogen carbonate is heated? Write the chemical equation. (2 marks)
  • Q3.Explain the preparation of washing soda from baking soda. Why is washing soda used as a cleansing agent? (3 marks)

Chapter 4: Carbon and Its Compounds

Very High Priority

5-mark question from this chapter is almost guaranteed every year

  • Q1.What is catenation? Why does carbon show this property more than silicon? (2 marks)
  • Q2.Name the functional group present in: (a) CH₃OH (b) CH₃COOH (c) CH₃CHO (d) CH₃COCH₃. (2 marks)
  • Q3.Explain saponification. Write the chemical equation for the reaction of ethyl ethanoate with NaOH. (3 marks)
  • Q4.What are homologous series? List four characteristics and give an example series. (5 marks)

Biology — Important Questions

Chapter 6: Life Processes

Very High Priority

Highest-scoring biology chapter — contributes 10–12 marks every year

  • Q1.Draw a labelled diagram of the human digestive system. Explain the role of each organ. (5 marks)
  • Q2.What is the difference between aerobic and anaerobic respiration? Give the chemical equations for both. (3 marks)
  • Q3.Why is the rate of breathing in aquatic organisms much faster than in terrestrial organisms? (2 marks — MCQ and short both)
  • Q4.Explain double circulation. Why is it necessary in human beings? (3 marks — asked 2023, 2021, 2018)

Chapter 8: How Do Organisms Reproduce?

High Priority
  • Q1.Distinguish between sexual and asexual reproduction. Give two examples of each. (3 marks)
  • Q2.Draw a labelled diagram of the human female reproductive system and explain the menstrual cycle. (5 marks)
  • Q3.What is vegetative propagation? Give two advantages and two methods with examples. (3 marks)

Chapter 9: Heredity and Evolution

High Priority
  • Q1.In a cross between a pure tall pea plant (TT) and a pure dwarf pea plant (tt), find the F1 and F2 generation ratios using a Punnett square. (3 marks — appears almost every year)
  • Q2.What are homologous and analogous organs? Give two examples of each with significance in evolution. (3 marks)
  • Q3.How is sex determined in human beings? Explain with a diagram showing chromosomal combinations. (3 marks)

Assertion-Reason Questions (New Pattern — 2023 Onwards)

CBSE introduced Assertion-Reason (A-R) questions in Class 10 Science from the 2023 board exam onward. These carry 1 mark each but confuse many students. Here are the most commonly tested A-R pairs:

A-R Example 1 — Chemistry

Assertion (A): Metals are generally hard and have high melting points.
Reason (R): Metallic bonds are strong due to the sea of electrons holding metal cations together.

Answer: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

A-R Example 2 — Biology

Assertion (A): Organisms that reproduce sexually have better chances of survival in changing environments.
Reason (R): Sexual reproduction introduces variations through recombination of genetic material.

Answer: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

A-R Example 3 — Physics

Assertion (A): A concave mirror is used as a shaving mirror.
Reason (R): A concave mirror always forms a magnified image of an object placed in front of it.

Answer: A is true but R is false — magnification only occurs when object is within focal length.

Chapter Priority Summary

ChapterSubjectAvg MarksPriority
ElectricityPhysics9 marks🔴 Must Do
Life ProcessesBiology11 marks🔴 Must Do
Carbon & CompoundsChemistry8 marks🔴 Must Do
Chemical ReactionsChemistry7 marks🔴 Must Do
Heredity & EvolutionBiology7 marks🟠 High
Magnetic EffectsPhysics7 marks🟠 High
Light — Reflection & RefractionPhysics8 marks🔴 Must Do
Acids, Bases & SaltsChemistry6 marks🟠 High

Frequently Asked Questions

How many chapters can I skip for CBSE Class 10 Science 2026?

Technically, no chapter should be completely skipped since CBSE can pick questions from anywhere. However, based on our data, chapters like Sources of Energy, Our Environment, and Management of Natural Resources carry the lowest weightage (2–3 marks combined) and can be covered briefly rather than deeply.

What is the passing marks for CBSE Class 10 Science?

Students must score at least 33% in both theory (80 marks) and internal assessment (20 marks) separately. So you need at least 27/80 in the written exam and 7/20 in practicals to pass Science.

Are NCERT questions enough for Class 10 Science board exams?

NCERT in-text and exercise questions form the foundation, but CBSE frequently modifies question wording or adds application-based twists. Practising PYQs from the last 5 years on PrepOne fills this gap effectively — you'll encounter all NCERT variants plus the rephrased versions CBSE actually uses.

How are the 20 marks for internal assessment distributed?

The 20 internal marks are split as: Periodic Tests (10 marks), Practical/Lab Work (5 marks), and Portfolio/Subject Enrichment Activities (5 marks). These are awarded by your school and should not be neglected.

title: CBSE Class 12 Chemistry PYQ Analysis — Chapter-wise Trends & Must-Practice Questions
author: PrepOne Academic Team
seoDescription: Deep analysis of CBSE Class 12 Chemistry previous year questions (2015–2026). Chapter-wise marks distribution, most repeated question types, named reactions, and scoring strategy for 2027 board exams.
content:
CBSE doesn't reinvent Chemistry every year — it tests the same core concepts repeatedly, just with a slightly different framing. If you analyse the last 10 years of Class 12 Chemistry board papers, a very clear pattern emerges: just 10 chapters drive all 70 marks, and within those, certain question types appear almost without fail. This is PrepOne's deep-dive PYQ analysis to help you prepare smarter for 2027.

📊 PrepOne Analysis: The CBSE Class 12 Chemistry 2026 paper was rated moderate in difficulty — Physical Chemistry was the most time-consuming section, while MCQs ranged from easy to moderate. Students who practised PYQs chapter-wise found the paper highly manageable.

CBSE Class 12 Chemistry — Exam Pattern (2026 Onwards)

The paper structure has been consistent since 2023 and is expected to continue for 2027:

SectionQuestion TypeNo. of QuestionsTotal Marks
AMCQs (incl. Assertion-Reason)1616
BVery Short Answer (2 marks)510
CShort Answer (3 marks)721
DCase-Based Questions (4 marks)28
ELong Answer (5 marks)315

Chapter-wise Marks Distribution (10-Year Average)

This is the most important table for your preparation. Every chapter below is part of the current reduced CBSE syllabus — and every single one appears in the board paper each year:

ChapterCategoryAvg MarksPriority
ElectrochemistryPhysical8–9🔴 Must Do
Aldehydes, Ketones & Carboxylic AcidsOrganic8🔴 Must Do
Chemical KineticsPhysical7🔴 Must Do
SolutionsPhysical7🔴 Must Do
Coordination CompoundsInorganic7🔴 Must Do
d- and f-Block ElementsInorganic7🔴 Must Do
BiomoleculesOrganic7🟠 High
AminesOrganic6🟠 High
Haloalkanes & HaloarenesOrganic6🟠 High
Surface ChemistryPhysical3–4🟡 Moderate

Physical Chemistry — PYQ Breakdown

Physical Chemistry is the most predictable section of the paper. Numerical patterns repeat almost identically across years — only the values change.

Electrochemistry

8–9 marks every year

Most marks-dense chapter in the entire paper. Never skips a year.

  • Q→Calculate the EMF of a cell using the Nernst equation: Zn | Zn²⁺ (0.001M) || Cu²⁺ (0.1M) | Cu. (3 marks — appears 8 of last 10 years)
  • Q→What is molar conductivity? How does it vary with concentration for strong and weak electrolytes? Draw graphs. (3 marks)
  • Q→Using Faraday's law, calculate the mass of silver deposited when 0.5A of current is passed for 2 hours through AgNO₃ solution. (2 marks — numerical)
  • MCQ→Identify cathode/anode in a given electrochemical cell. Watch for sign-based traps in electron flow direction. (1 mark)

Chemical Kinetics

7 marks every year
  • Q→Derive the integrated rate equation for a first-order reaction. What is its half-life? (3 marks — asked 7 of last 10 years)
  • Q→The rate constant of a first-order reaction is 2.0 × 10⁻³ min⁻¹. Calculate the time for 40% decomposition. (2 marks)
  • Q→Define activation energy. How does a catalyst affect the activation energy? Show with a potential energy diagram. (3 marks)

Solutions

7 marks every year
  • Q→State Raoult's law. Explain positive and negative deviations with examples. (3 marks)
  • Q→What is Van't Hoff factor? Calculate the depression in freezing point of a solution containing 5g of NaCl in 200g water (Kf = 1.86 K kg mol⁻¹). (3 marks)
  • MCQ→Which of the following shows maximum elevation in boiling point — NaCl, glucose, MgCl₂ or AlCl₃ (equimolar solutions)? Requires Van't Hoff factor logic. (1 mark)

Organic Chemistry — PYQ Breakdown

Organic Chemistry tests reaction logic and mechanism clarity, not just product memory. In 2026, the 5-mark Organic question came from Alcohols & Phenols instead of the expected Carbonyl chapter — a reminder that you must cover all organic chapters.

Aldehydes, Ketones & Carboxylic Acids

8 marks — highest organic chapter
  • Q→Explain Aldol condensation with mechanism. What happens when acetaldehyde undergoes cross-Aldol with benzaldehyde? (3 marks)
  • Q→Distinguish between propanal and propanone using (a) Tollens' reagent (b) Fehling's solution (c) Iodoform test. (3 marks)
  • Q→What is Cannizzaro reaction? Which aldehydes undergo it and why? (2 marks — tests alpha-hydrogen logic)

Amines

6 marks
  • Q→Arrange the following in increasing order of basic strength: Aniline, Methylamine, Dimethylamine, Ammonia. Justify your answer. (3 marks — asked every other year)
  • Q→What is the Hoffmann Bromamide degradation reaction? Write the mechanism with reagents. (2 marks)
  • Q→How is benzene diazonium chloride prepared from aniline? Give two reactions of diazonium salts (Sandmeyer reaction). (3 marks)

Haloalkanes & Haloarenes + Alcohols & Phenols

6–7 marks combined
  • Q→Explain SN1 and SN2 mechanisms with one example each. Why does tert-butyl bromide prefer SN1? (3 marks)
  • Q→Give Williamson synthesis to prepare anisole from phenol. (2 marks)
  • Q→Why is phenol more acidic than ethanol but less acidic than carbonic acid? (2 marks — reasoning type)

Inorganic Chemistry — PYQ Breakdown

Inorganic rewards precision over length. Short, NCERT-aligned answers score full marks. Long explanations do not earn extra credit here.

Coordination Compounds

7 marks every year
  • Q→Write the IUPAC name, magnetic moment, and hybridisation of [Fe(CN)₆]³⁻ and [Fe(H₂O)₆]³⁺. (3 marks — asked 6 of last 10 years)
  • Q→Explain with examples: (a) linkage isomerism (b) ionisation isomerism. (2 marks)
  • MCQ→A complex [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]⁺ shows two isomers. Identify the type of isomerism. (1 mark)

d- and f-Block Elements

7 marks
  • Q→Why do transition metals show variable oxidation states? Give examples for Mn showing maximum oxidation states. (3 marks)
  • Q→Why are transition metal compounds generally coloured? Which transition metal ion is colourless and why? (2 marks)
  • A-R→Assertion: Cu²⁺ is more stable than Cu⁺ in aqueous solution. Reason: Cu²⁺ has higher hydration enthalpy that compensates for its higher ionisation energy. (1 mark — frequently tested)

Named Reactions That Repeat Every 1–2 Years

These named reactions have appeared so consistently in PYQs that missing even one is a strategic error:

Aldol Condensation

Requires alpha-hydrogen; cross-Aldol framing common in 3-mark format

Cannizzaro Reaction

Aldehydes with no alpha-H; disproportionation logic must be explained

Reimer–Tiemann Reaction

Phenol + CHCl₃ + NaOH → salicylaldehyde; mechanism optional but helpful

Hoffmann Bromamide Degradation

Amide → primary amine with one fewer carbon; conditions mandatory

Sandmeyer Reaction

Diazonium salt → haloarene using CuCl/CuBr; frequently a 2-mark sub-part

Williamson Synthesis

Ether preparation via SN2; mechanism and limitation questions common

Kolbe's Reaction

Phenol + CO₂ + NaOH → salicylic acid; often paired with Reimer-Tiemann

Gabriel Phthalimide Synthesis

Primary amine preparation; limitations asked in reasoning format

MCQ Traps CBSE Uses Repeatedly

Section A MCQs have become more conceptual since 2023. These are the most common trap areas PrepOne has identified across PYQs:

  • Electrochemistry: Cathode/anode identification — students confuse galvanic vs electrolytic cells
  • Solutions: Van't Hoff factor with association (e.g., acetic acid in benzene, i < 1) is frequently missed
  • Chemical Kinetics: Units of rate constant for zero-order vs first-order reactions — dimensional analysis required
  • Coordination Compounds: Magnetic moment calculation — count unpaired electrons carefully for Fe²⁺ vs Fe³⁺
  • Amines: Basic strength in aqueous phase vs gaseous phase gives opposite orders — the question context matters
  • Solid State: Frenkel defect vs Schottky defect — which compound shows which, and the density implication

2026 Paper Difficulty Snapshot

SectionDifficulty (2026 Actual)Key Observation
Section A — MCQsEasy–ModerateStraightforward; careful reading needed for A-R
Section B — VSAModerate–HardNot always direct; required analytical thinking
Section C — SAEasy–ModerateMostly numerical-based; familiar patterns
Section D — Case-BasedModerate–HardRequired application, not just recall
Section E — LAEasy–ModerateLengthy but formula-driven; Physical Chem heavy

Source: Expert analysis from CBSE 2026 paper review. Expected good score: 55+/70.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which section of Class 12 Chemistry is the most time-consuming in the board exam?

Physical Chemistry — particularly Section C and Section E — is consistently the most time-consuming. Numerical problems in Electrochemistry and Chemical Kinetics require multi-step working. Allocate roughly 35–40 minutes for these sections in your exam strategy.

Are NCERT examples enough for scoring 90+ in Chemistry?

NCERT forms the foundation, but CBSE increasingly modifies question framing to test application. Students who only memorise NCERT answers without practising PYQs often struggle with Section B and Section D. A combination of NCERT + last 8 years of PYQs is the recommended approach for 90+ scores.

Which organic chemistry chapters are most important for 2027?

Aldehydes, Ketones & Carboxylic Acids consistently contribute the most marks in Organic (8 marks average). Amines and Haloalkanes follow. In 2026, the 5-mark question came from Alcohols & Phenols — a reminder that you should not skip any organic chapter entirely.

How many PYQ years should I solve for Chemistry?

At minimum, solve the last 8 years. Prioritise 2020–2026 since that period reflects the current competency-based question format. On PrepOne, you can filter PYQs chapter-wise and by question type (MCQ, numerical, reasoning) for targeted practice.

title: CBSE Class 12 Maths PYQ Analysis — Chapter-wise Marks & Most Repeated Questions
author: PrepOne Academic Team
seoDescription: Deep analysis of CBSE Class 12 Maths previous year questions (2015–2026). Chapter-wise marks weightage, most repeated integration types, probability models, and scoring strategy for 2027 board exams.
content:
CBSE Class 12 Maths is the most marks-critical subject for Science stream students — a single paper that can swing your aggregate by 20–30 marks. The good news: it is the most predictable paper in the entire board exam. PrepOne's analysis of 10 years of CBSE Maths papers reveals that the same question structures repeat year after year, just with different numbers.

📊 PrepOne Analysis: Over 65% of marks in CBSE Class 12 Maths come from Calculus alone. Students who master Integration, Differential Equations, and Applications of Derivatives can realistically score 80+ out of 80 in those sections.

CBSE Class 12 Maths — Exam Pattern 2026

SectionQuestion TypeNo. of QuestionsTotal Marks
AMCQs + Assertion-Reason (1 mark each)2020
BVery Short Answer (2 marks)510
CShort Answer (3 marks)618
DLong Answer (5 marks)420
ECase-Based (4 marks each)312

Chapter-wise Marks Distribution (10-Year Average)

ChapterUnitAvg MarksPriority
Integrals (Definite + Indefinite)Calculus16–18🔴 Must Do
Applications of DerivativesCalculus10🔴 Must Do
Differential EquationsCalculus8🔴 Must Do
Vectors & 3D GeometryVectors & 3D14🔴 Must Do
ProbabilityStatistics8🔴 Must Do
Matrices & DeterminantsAlgebra10🔴 Must Do
Relations & FunctionsAlgebra5🟠 High
Inverse TrigonometryAlgebra4🟡 Moderate
Linear ProgrammingLPP5🟠 High

Calculus — PYQ Deep Dive

Integrals (Definite + Indefinite)

16–18 marks every year

The single biggest chapter in CBSE Maths. A 5-mark and two 3-mark questions are almost guaranteed.

  • Q→Evaluate: ∫ x² / (x⁴ + x² + 1) dx using partial fractions. (5 marks — appears every 2 years)
  • Q→Using definite integration, find the area bounded by the curve y = x² and the line y = x + 2. (5 marks — area between curves)
  • Q→Evaluate: ∫₀^π x sin x / (1 + cos²x) dx using the king property. (3 marks — property-based; asked 2023, 2022, 2020)
  • Q→Evaluate: ∫ e^x (sin x + cos x) dx. (2 marks — standard e^x formula)

Applications of Derivatives

10 marks every year
  • Q→A ladder 5m long rests against a vertical wall. If its bottom slides away at 2 cm/s, find the rate at which the top slides down when the foot is 4m from the wall. (3 marks — rate of change)
  • Q→Find the local maxima and minima of f(x) = x³ − 6x² + 9x + 15. Using first/second derivative test. (5 marks)
  • Q→Show that of all rectangles inscribed in a given circle, the square has the maximum area. (5 marks — optimisation)

Differential Equations

8 marks
  • Q→Solve: dy/dx + y cot x = 2 cos x. Find the particular solution given y = 0 when x = π/2. (5 marks — Linear DE; asked every 2 years)
  • Q→Form the differential equation representing the family of curves y = a sin(x + b), where a, b are arbitrary constants. (3 marks)

Vectors & 3D Geometry — PYQ Deep Dive

Vectors

Embedded in 14-mark combined unit
  • Q→If |a⃗| = 3, |b⃗| = √2, and a⃗ · b⃗ = √6, find the angle between a⃗ and b⃗. (2 marks)
  • Q→Find the area of a parallelogram whose adjacent sides are a⃗ = 2î + ĵ + k̂ and b⃗ = î − ĵ. (2 marks — cross product application)
  • Q→Prove that [a⃗ + b⃗, b⃗ + c⃗, c⃗ + a⃗] = 2[a⃗, b⃗, c⃗] using scalar triple product. (3 marks)

3D Geometry

5-mark question guaranteed
  • Q→Find the shortest distance between the lines: r⃗ = (î + 2ĵ − k̂) + λ(2î − ĵ + k̂) and r⃗ = (2î − ĵ − k̂) + μ(3î − 5ĵ + 2k̂). (5 marks — skew lines; asked 2024, 2022, 2020)
  • Q→Find the equation of the plane passing through three points A(1,1,0), B(1,2,1), C(−2,2,−1). (3 marks)

Algebra & Probability — PYQ Deep Dive

Matrices & Determinants

10 marks
  • Q→Using elementary row operations, find the inverse of: [1 2 3; 2 5 7; 3 1 2]. (5 marks — asked 7 of last 10 years)
  • Q→If A = [3 1; −1 2], show that A² − 5A + 7I = 0 and use it to find A⁻¹. (5 marks — Cayley-Hamilton type)
  • A-R→Assertion: The number of all possible matrices of order 3×3 with each entry 0 or 1 is 512. Reason: There are 9 entries and each can take 2 values. (1 mark)

Probability

8 marks every year
  • Q→A bag contains 3 red and 4 black balls. Two balls are drawn at random without replacement. Find the probability distribution of the number of red balls drawn. (5 marks — PD table; appears every 2 years)
  • Q→A pair of dice is thrown 4 times. Find the probability of getting a doublet exactly 2 times using Binomial distribution. (3 marks)
  • Q→Using Bayes' theorem: A manufacturer has machines A and B producing 60% and 40% of items respectively. Machine A produces 2% defectives and B produces 5% defectives. An item is drawn at random and found defective. Find the probability it came from machine B. (3 marks — Bayes; asked 2024, 2022, 2021)

Integration Formulas That Appear Every Year

These standard integration types are consistently tested in Sections B and C. If you can solve these within 2 minutes each, you're in the 90+ bracket:

Integration by Parts

∫ x eˣ dx, ∫ x² log x dx — ILATE priority rule. 2–3 marks.

Partial Fractions

Rational functions with repeated/quadratic denominators. 3–5 marks.

King Property of Definite Integrals

∫₀^a f(x) dx = ∫₀^a f(a−x) dx. Simplifies complex definite integrals.

Substitution — t = tan(x/2)

Trigonometric integrals of the form 1/(a + b sinx). Appears as 3-mark question.

eˣ[f(x) + f′(x)] Formula

∫ eˣ(f(x) + f′(x)) dx = eˣ f(x) + C. Quick 2-mark question.

Area Under Curve

Parabola, ellipse, and line-curve combinations. 5-mark long answer type.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is NCERT enough for scoring 90+ in CBSE Class 12 Maths?

NCERT exercises and examples cover about 80% of what CBSE asks. The remaining 20% — particularly case-based questions and application problems — require exposure to PYQs. Solve all NCERT examples first, then use PrepOne's PYQ filter for the exact question types CBSE has used in the past 8 years.

How much time should I spend on Calculus vs. other chapters?

Calculus accounts for approximately 44 marks out of 80. Ideally, 50–60% of your practice time should be on Calculus chapters. Among them, prioritise Integrals (16 marks) first, then Applications of Derivatives (10 marks), and then Differential Equations (8 marks).

What type of questions appear in the Case-Based section for Maths?

Case-based questions (Section E) typically present a real-world scenario — building design, water tank, traffic model — and ask you to apply concepts from Calculus (rate of change, optimisation) or Probability (Bayes theorem, probability distribution). They appear straightforward but require careful reading. Each case has 4 sub-parts worth 1 mark each.

title: BSEB Class 12 Previous Year Question Papers PDF (2016–2026) — All Subjects
author: PrepOne Academic Team
seoDescription: Download Bihar Board (BSEB) Class 12 previous year question papers from 2016 to 2026 for Science, Commerce, and Arts streams. Free PDF with answer keys and chapter-wise analysis.
content:
The Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) Intermediate exam is one of the largest board exams in India, with over 13 lakh students appearing each year. If you are preparing for BSEB Class 12 in 2027, practising previous year question papers from 2016 to 2026 is the single most effective strategy available. This guide covers everything about BSEB PYQs — what to download, where, and how to use them strategically.

📌 BSEB Trend Alert 2026: The 2026 exam saw increased focus on application-based questions in Science stream, especially in Physics and Chemistry. Rote-memorised answers scored less than in previous years — practising PYQs with PrepOne's AI explanation feature prepares you for this shift.

BSEB Class 12 PYQs — Subject Availability

SubjectStreamYears AvailableAnswer Key
PhysicsScience2016–2026✓ Full
ChemistryScience2016–2026✓ Full
MathematicsScience / Commerce2016–2026✓ Full
BiologyScience2016–2026✓ Full
HindiAll Streams2018–2026Partial
EnglishAll Streams2018–2026Partial
AccountancyCommerce2019–2026✓ Full
EconomicsArts / Commerce2019–2026✓ Full

Why BSEB PYQs Are Different from CBSE

Unlike CBSE, BSEB does not publish a separate marking scheme document for each paper. The official answer key released after the exam functions as the marking reference. This means BSEB PYQs must be analysed differently — what matters is the expected answer length and the key terms used in the official key, not step-by-step marking breakdowns.

  • BSEB has a higher objective question weightage (50% MCQs since 2025) compared to CBSE
  • BSEB subjective answers are expected to be more concise — 3-mark answers in 3–4 sentences, not half a page
  • BSEB repeats exact questions verbatim more frequently than CBSE — practising PYQs gives higher direct repeat probability
  • BSEB Physics and Chemistry questions are closer to NCERT examples than CBSE, which modifies application context

Most Repeated BSEB Questions (2016–2026)

Physics — Repeat Frequency Champions

  • →State Gauss's theorem and derive the expression for electric field due to an infinite plane sheet — appeared 8/10 years
  • →What is potentiometer? How is it used to compare EMF of two cells? — appeared 7/10 years
  • →State Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction with one application — appeared 9/10 years
  • →Derive lens maker's formula — appeared 6/10 years

Chemistry — Repeat Frequency Champions

  • →Derive integrated rate equation for first-order reaction — appeared 8/10 years
  • →What is Nernst equation? Calculate EMF of a given cell — appeared 7/10 years
  • →Explain coordination isomerism and ionisation isomerism with examples — appeared 6/10 years
  • →Raoult's Law — statement, deviations, azeotropes — appeared 7/10 years

Mathematics — Repeat Frequency Champions

  • →Find the inverse of a 3×3 matrix using row operations — appeared 9/10 years
  • →Evaluate a definite integral using properties — appeared every year
  • →Probability distribution table + mean and variance — appeared 8/10 years
  • →Shortest distance between skew lines in 3D — appeared 6/10 years

BSEB vs CBSE PYQ Practice — Key Differences

AspectBSEBCBSE
Objective question % 50% (since 2025)25–30%
Verbatim question repeat rateHigh (40–50%)Moderate (20–30%)
Case-based questionsNot yet includedMandatory since 2023
Official answer key detailBrief key onlyFull step-by-step marking scheme
Assertion-Reason questionsRareIncluded since 2023

Frequently Asked Questions

Where can I get BSEB 2026 question paper PDF?

BSEB releases official question papers on biharboardonline.bihar.gov.in after the exam. PrepOne hosts all available BSEB PYQs from 2016–2026 in a chapter-tagged, searchable format — you can filter by subject, year, and topic directly in the app.

Is the BSEB 2027 exam pattern changing?

BSEB has confirmed the 50% objective / 50% subjective pattern introduced in 2025 will continue in 2027. No major structural changes have been announced. PrepOne will update mock tests and PYQ collections as soon as any official notification is released.

How many BSEB PYQs should I solve before the exam?

Aim to solve at least the last 5 years of full papers per subject (2022–2026). For chapters with high repeat rates (like Chemical Kinetics, Matrix Inverse, and Electromagnetic Induction), go back to 2016. On PrepOne, you can filter chapter-wise PYQs and track which ones you've solved.

title: CBSE Class 10 Maths Important Questions 2026 — Chapter-wise with Marks Breakdown
author: PrepOne Academic Team
seoDescription: Chapter-wise most important questions for CBSE Class 10 Maths 2026 board exam. Covers Real Numbers, Triangles, Arithmetic Progressions, Probability with expected 3-mark and 5-mark questions.
content:
CBSE Class 10 Maths is a subject where the difference between a 75 and a 95 is almost entirely about knowing which questions to practise. The syllabus is fixed, the marks distribution is predictable, and CBSE recycles question types year after year. This guide gives you the chapter-wise most important questions for the 2026 board exam, validated against 10 years of CBSE PYQs.

📊 PrepOne Data: 5 chapters — Triangles, Arithmetic Progressions, Coordinate Geometry, Quadratic Equations, and Probability — contribute an average of 52 marks out of 80 in CBSE Class 10 Maths. Master these first.

CBSE Class 10 Maths — Exam Pattern 2026

SectionTypeMarks
AMCQs + Assertion-Reason (1 mark each × 20)20
BVery Short Answer (2 marks × 5)10
CShort Answer (3 marks × 6)18
DLong Answer (5 marks × 4)20
ECase-Based (4 marks × 3)12

Chapter-wise Important Questions

Chapter 5: Arithmetic Progressions (AP)

Very High Priority — 8–10 marks
  • Q1.The sum of the first n terms of an AP is 5n² − 3n. Find the AP and the 10th term. (3 marks — appears almost every year)
  • Q2.How many three-digit numbers are divisible by 7? Find their sum. (3 marks)
  • Q3.The 4th term of an AP is 0. Prove that its 25th term is triple its 11th term. (3 marks)
  • MCQ→If the nth term of an AP is (2n + 1), then the sum of its first n terms is: (a) n(n+2) (b) n(n+1) (c) n² + 2n (d) n² + n. (1 mark)

Chapter 6: Triangles

Very High Priority — 8–10 marks

Proofs from this chapter appear in the 5-mark section almost every year. Cannot be skipped.

  • Q1.Prove that if a line is drawn parallel to one side of a triangle to intersect the other two sides at distinct points, the other two sides are divided in the same ratio (BPT). (5 marks — proof)
  • Q2.In △ABC, D is on BC such that AD ⊥ BC. Prove that AB² + CD² = AC² + BD². (3 marks)
  • Q3.Sides of two similar triangles are in ratio 4:9. Find the ratio of their areas. (2 marks)

Chapter 4: Quadratic Equations

Very High Priority — 7–8 marks
  • Q1.A train travels 360 km at a uniform speed. If the speed had been 5 km/h more, it would have taken 1 hour less. Find the speed. (4 marks — word problem; appears every 2 years)
  • Q2.Find the value of k for which the quadratic equation 2x² + kx + 3 = 0 has two equal roots. (2 marks — discriminant)
  • Q3.Solve: 1/(x−1) + 2/(x−2) = 3/(x−3). (3 marks)

Chapter 7: Coordinate Geometry

High Priority — 6–7 marks
  • Q1.Find the ratio in which the line segment joining (4, −3) and (8, 5) is divided by the point (7, 3). (2 marks — section formula)
  • Q2.Find the area of a triangle whose vertices are (1, −1), (−4, 6), and (−3, −5). (3 marks — coordinate area formula)
  • Q3.Prove that the points (−2, −1), (1, 0), (4, 3), and (1, 2) are the vertices of a parallelogram. (3 marks)

Chapter 12 & 13: Areas Related to Circles + Surface Areas & Volumes

Very High Priority — 10–12 marks combined
  • Q1.A sector of a circle of radius 12 cm has angle 120°. Find the area of the corresponding major segment. (3 marks)
  • Q2.A hemispherical depression is cut out from one face of a cubical wooden block of side 7 cm. Find the surface area of the remaining solid. (5 marks — composite solid)
  • Q3.Water flows through a cylindrical pipe of diameter 1.4 cm at 2 m/s. How much time will it take to fill a conical vessel of diameter 40 cm and depth 24 cm? (4 marks — rate of flow)

Chapter 15: Probability

High Priority — 5–6 marks
  • Q1.Two dice are thrown simultaneously. Find the probability of getting (i) a doublet (ii) a sum of 8 (iii) a sum greater than 10. (3 marks — asked 2024, 2022, 2019)
  • Q2.Cards numbered 1 to 50 are placed in a box. A card is drawn at random. Find the probability that the card has (i) a prime number (ii) a multiple of 8. (2 marks)

Case-Based Questions — What to Expect

Since 2023, Section E of CBSE Class 10 Maths contains 3 case-based questions (4 marks each = 12 marks total). These consistently come from:

  • Arithmetic Progressions — seating arrangement, salary increments, savings patterns (appears ~2 years of 3)
  • Statistics — frequency distribution table, mean/mode from real-world data
  • Coordinate Geometry — map-based problems, distance between landmarks
  • Areas and Volumes — real-world objects like tents, wells, tanks

Sample Case-Based Question (AP Type)

A school is organising a marathon. The first lap is 100m, and each subsequent lap is 50m longer than the previous one. Based on this information, answer the following:

  1. Write the AP formed by the lap lengths. (1 mark)
  2. Find the length of the 10th lap. (1 mark)
  3. If a student completes 8 laps, find the total distance covered. (2 marks)

Frequently Asked Questions

Which chapters can be skipped in CBSE Class 10 Maths 2026?

No chapter can be completely skipped, but Polynomials (Chapter 2) and Introduction to Trigonometry (Chapter 8) are relatively low-weightage standalone chapters. However, Trigonometric identities feed into application-based problems in Chapter 9 (Heights and Distances), so don't skip core formulas.

How is Section D (5-mark questions) evaluated?

5-mark questions in Maths are heavily step-marked. Typically: identifying the method (1 mark) + correct setup (1 mark) + intermediate working (2 marks) + final answer (1 mark). Even an incorrect final answer with correct working can score 3–4 out of 5. Always show all steps.

Are the proofs in Triangles chapter guaranteed in the exam?

Yes. One theorem proof from the Triangles chapter has appeared in every CBSE Class 10 Maths paper for the last 10 consecutive years. The Basic Proportionality Theorem (BPT) and Pythagoras Theorem are the two most frequently asked. Know both proofs completely.

title: BSEB Class 10 Matric Important Questions 2026 — Science & Maths Chapter-wise
author: Anmol Kumar
seoDescription: Most important questions for BSEB Class 10 Matric 2026 exam in Science and Maths. Chapter-wise expected questions with marks distribution for Bihar Board 10th board preparation.
content:
Bihar Board Class 10 (Matric) is the gateway exam for over 16 lakh students every year. The BSEB Matric exam has a distinct pattern — higher direct question repetition than CBSE, a large objective section, and a syllabus aligned closely with NCERT. Students who target the right chapters consistently outperform those who study uniformly across all topics. Here is your complete chapter-wise important question guide for 2026.

⚠️ 2026 BSEB Pattern: BSEB Matric now has 50% objective (MCQ/fill-in-the-blank) and 50% subjective questions. Objective section has no negative marking — attempt all MCQs without hesitation.

Science — Chapter-wise Important Questions

Chapter 1: Chemical Reactions and Equations

Very High Priority
  • Q1.What are the types of chemical reactions? Give one example and chemical equation for each type. (5 marks — 2024, 2022, 2019)
  • Q2.Define decomposition reaction. Distinguish between thermal, electrolytic, and photolytic decomposition. (3 marks)
  • MCQ→Which of the following is a double displacement reaction? (a) CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂ (b) Zn + H₂SO₄ → ZnSO₄ + H₂ (c) Na₂SO₄ + BaCl₂ → BaSO₄↓ + 2NaCl (d) 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O (1 mark)

Chapter 13: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current

Very High Priority
  • Q1.Describe the construction and working of an electric motor with a labelled diagram. (5 marks — appeared 2016, 2018, 2020, 2022, 2024)
  • Q2.State Fleming's left hand rule and right hand rule. When is each used? (2 marks)
  • Q3.What are the domestic uses of electromagnetic induction? Name the device. (2 marks)

Chapter 6: Life Processes

Very High Priority
  • Q1.Draw and explain the human digestive system. Name the enzyme secreted at each stage. (5 marks)
  • Q2.What is photosynthesis? Write the overall equation. Name any two factors affecting the rate of photosynthesis. (3 marks)
  • Q3.Why does the left ventricle have a thicker wall than the right ventricle? (2 marks — reasoning type)

Chapter 9: Heredity and Evolution

High Priority
  • Q1.Cross a homozygous tall pea plant (TT) with a dwarf pea plant (tt). Show the F1 and F2 generation using Punnett square and state the genotypic and phenotypic ratios. (5 marks)
  • Q2.What is natural selection? How did it lead to the industrial melanism of Biston betularia? (3 marks)

Mathematics — Chapter-wise Important Questions

Real Numbers & Polynomials

High Priority — 6 marks
  • Q1.Find the HCF and LCM of 404 and 96 using the prime factorisation method. Verify: HCF × LCM = product of the numbers. (3 marks)
  • Q2.If α and β are the zeroes of the polynomial p(x) = 2x² + 7x + 3, find α + β, αβ, and 1/α + 1/β. (3 marks)

Quadratic Equations & AP

Very High Priority — 10+ marks
  • Q1.The sum of squares of two consecutive even natural numbers is 340. Find the numbers. (3 marks — word problem)
  • Q2.Find the 20th term and the sum of the first 20 terms of the AP: 2, 7, 12, 17… (3 marks)
  • Q3.The 7th term of an AP is 32 and its 13th term is 62. Find the AP. (3 marks — standard type, appears every year)

Triangles & Circles

Very High Priority — 10–12 marks
  • Q1.Prove: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact. (5 marks — guaranteed every 2 years)
  • Q2.Two tangents are drawn from an external point to a circle. Prove they are equal in length. (3 marks — appears 7/10 years)
  • Q3.Prove BPT: If a line is drawn parallel to one side of a triangle, it divides the other two sides in the same ratio. (5 marks)

BSEB Matric — Chapter Priority at a Glance

ChapterSubjectAvg MarksRepeat Rate
Life ProcessesScience10–12Every year
Magnetic Effects of CurrentScience8–10Every year
Quadratic EquationsMaths8Every year
Arithmetic ProgressionsMaths8Every year
CirclesMaths88/10 years
Chemical ReactionsScience7–8Every year

Frequently Asked Questions

When is BSEB Matric 2027 exam?

BSEB Matric (Class 10) exams are traditionally held in February. The exact schedule for 2027 will be released on biharboardonline.bihar.gov.in by October–November 2026. PrepOne will send in-app notifications as soon as dates are official.

Does BSEB Matric have negative marking in objective questions?

No. BSEB Matric objective questions carry no negative marking. This means you should attempt every single objective question — a blank is always worse than a guess. For 1-mark MCQs, if you can eliminate 2 options, your probability of guessing correctly is 50%.

How many marks are needed for First Division in BSEB Matric?

First Division in BSEB Matric requires 45% aggregate. Second Division is 33–44.9%. Distinction is awarded above 75% in all subjects (varies by school). Scoring 70%+ in Science and Maths significantly boosts aggregate.
